Xizang Zhihui Mining Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2546) ) has provided an update.

Shareholders of Xizang Zhihui Mining Co., Ltd. approved all resolutions at the annual general meeting held in Hangzhou on May 29, 2026, with about 75% of issued shares represented. The meeting complied with PRC company law and Hong Kong listing rules, and all directors participated either in person or electronically.

Investors backed the 2025 annual report, board report, profit distribution plan, auditor reappointment, changes to executive director remuneration, and mandates to issue and repurchase H shares, as well as amendments to the Articles of Association. The unanimous support consolidates the board’s authority on capital management and governance, providing flexibility for future share issuances and buy-backs that could affect capital structure and shareholder returns.

More about Xizang Zhihui Mining Co., Ltd. Class H

Xizang Zhihui Mining Co., Ltd. is a Chinese joint stock company engaged in the mining industry and is listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2546. The company has both domestic shares and H shares, reflecting a focus on the mainland Chinese market while also accessing international capital via Hong Kong.

data.title: Vala Inc. Sets 2026 AGM to Renew Share Issuance Mandate and Confirm Governance
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

51 Credit Card Inc. ( (HK:2051) ) has shared an update.

Vala Inc. has convened its annual general meeting for 24 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and approve the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the related directors’ and auditor’s reports. The meeting will also consider the re-election of executive director Sun Haitao and independent non-executive director Xu Xuchu, authorize the board to set directors’ remuneration, re-appoint Zhonghui Anda CPA Limited as auditor, and approve a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, excluding certain standard exceptions.

These resolutions, if passed, will refresh the company’s authorization to raise equity capital and manage its share base flexibly, including issuing new shares under a capped general mandate in line with Hong Kong listing rules. The governance items, such as director re-elections and auditor re-appointment, underscore continuity in oversight while preserving the board’s ability to respond to future financing or strategic needs, with implications for potential share dilution and capital structure management closely watched by investors.

More about 51 Credit Card Inc.

Vala Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2051. It operates under the Hong Kong listing regime and is governed by a board of directors that includes executive and independent non-executive members, reflecting standard corporate governance practices for Hong Kong–listed issuers.

IRICO Group New Energy Company Limited has called its 2025 annual general meeting for 29 June 2026 in Xianyang, Shaanxi Province, where shareholders will review the board of directors’ report and the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025. This mandate, covering both domestic shares and H shares, would permit the board to issue, allot, and deal in additional shares or related instruments during a defined period, potentially enhancing the company’s financial flexibility for future capital-raising or strategic initiatives. data.description:

Hong Kong Finance Group Ltd. ( (HK:1273) ) has provided an update.

Hong Kong Finance Group has agreed to extend a new secured loan of HK$4 million to three repeat customers under a nine-month facility carrying an interest rate of 18% per annum. The loan is backed by a second mortgage over two residential units and two car parking spaces on Conduit Road, Hong Kong, valued at about HK$148 million by an independent valuer.

Including this advance, the group has provided a total of HK$60 million in loans to these borrowers within a year, requiring aggregation under Hong Kong listing rules and triggering a discloseable transaction classification. The company says the short tenure, strong collateral coverage, and solid financial background and repayment history of key borrowers render the credit risk manageable for its lending portfolio.

More about Hong Kong Finance Group Ltd.

Hong Kong Finance Group Limited is a Hong Kong-based financial services company engaged in money lending activities through its subsidiary HK Finance. The group focuses on providing secured loans to customers, often backed by real estate collateral in prime locations, and operates under the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has provided an announcement.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that it paid coupon income on its 001PC-05 series non-documentary commercial bonds maturing in September 2027, issued under its 001РС commercial bonds programme. The bonds, with a nominal value of 1,000 rubles each and a total of 30 million outstanding, carry ISIN RU000A10B065.

For the fifteenth coupon period running from April 28 to May 28, 2026, the company paid a total of 374.4 million rubles in interest to bondholders. The coupon amounted to an annualized rate of 15.18%, equivalent to 12.48 rubles per bond, and was settled in non-cash Russian ruble payments on May 28, 2026, underscoring RUSAL’s ongoing servicing of its domestic debt obligations.

United Company RUSAL is a major international aluminium producer, operating as a public joint-stock company incorporated in Jersey and continued in the Russian Federation. The company is listed in Hong Kong, Moscow and St. Petersburg, providing investors with exposure to the global aluminium market and related financial instruments.

data.title: Redsun Services Flags Disputed Shareholder Meeting, Board Changes and Trading Suspension
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The latest update is out from Redsun Services Group Ltd ( (HK:1971) ).

Redsun Services Group Limited has disclosed that receivers and requisitioning shareholders have sent the company purported minutes and voting results from an adjourned extraordinary general meeting held on 28 May 2026, claiming that all proposed resolutions were passed. The resolutions include the removal of executive director Jia Hongbo and three independent non-executive directors, and the appointment of Tai Shaw Hoong as an executive director, all purportedly approved with 100% of votes cast in favour and none against.

The board highlights that there are serious questions over the validity of the entire extraordinary general meeting process, including its adjournment and the documents used to convene it, which may render the proceedings and the claimed resolutions invalid. In light of this corporate governance dispute and continuing uncertainty around control and board composition, trading in the company’s shares has been suspended, affecting shareholders and potential investors while the situation is clarified.

More about Redsun Services Group Ltd

Redsun Services Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating through subsidiaries as part of the broader property and services sector. Its shares are traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 1971, and its shareholder base includes parties that have recently enforced security interests over pledged shares.

Average Trading Volume: 75,516

data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd. ( (HK:1895) ) has provided an announcement.

Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd. has removed Mr. Wang Yantao as president of its property management department, effective 29 May 2026, and the board said the change is not expected to adversely affect the group’s operations. The move follows the board’s finding that, during his tenure, Mr. Wang took part in external communication activities in which materials were released without proper internal authorization, prompting the company to clarify that any such unauthorized communications do not represent its views and to pledge further strengthening of governance and authorization systems to protect shareholder interests.

The company stressed that only formally authorized statements, meeting records, and communications reflect its official positions, distancing itself from any prior unauthorized disclosures linked to Mr. Wang’s activities. It reiterated its commitment to corporate governance and internal control and said it will continue reviewing and tightening internal governance and authorization mechanisms, signalling to investors and other stakeholders that it aims to contain potential reputational or compliance risks arising from unauthorized public communications.

More about Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd.

Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd. is a Hong Kong–listed property management group incorporated in the Cayman Islands. The company focuses on providing property management and related services, operating through a group structure that serves property owners and communities, with an emphasis on corporate governance and internal control in its management practices.

An update from COSCO SHIPPING International (Hong Kong) Co ( (HK:0517) ) is now available.

COSCO SHIPPING International (Hong Kong) Co., Ltd., a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong and part of the wider COSCO SHIPPING group, acts as an investment and services arm with a diversified board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. The company’s shares are widely held, and its governance and capital structure are shaped through shareholder resolutions at its annual general meetings.

At its 29 May 2026 annual general meeting, shareholders approved all resolutions by wide margins, including the adoption of the 2025 audited accounts and the declaration of both a final and a special dividend. Investors also backed the re-election of director Simon X. Jiang, the reappointment of SHINEWING (HK) CPA Limited as auditor, and broad share issuance and repurchase mandates, reinforcing management’s financial flexibility and signalling continued shareholder support for the company’s current strategy and governance.

The poll results showed no mandated abstentions or declared opposition blocs under Hong Kong listing rules, indicating a largely unified shareholder base. The strong approval of capital-related mandates and board proposals suggests stable corporate control and may facilitate future funding or capital management actions, potentially influencing the company’s ability to respond swiftly to market opportunities and optimise returns for investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0517) stock is a Buy with a HK$12.90 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on COSCO SHIPPING International (Hong Kong) Co stock, see the HK:0517 Stock Forecast page.

More about COSCO SHIPPING International (Hong Kong) Co

COSCO SHIPPING International (Hong Kong) Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda and part of the COSCO SHIPPING group. The company operates as an investment holding and services platform for the group, with its shares traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, giving it access to international capital and a broad base of institutional and public shareholders.

The company’s governance structure includes a mix of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard Hong Kong corporate practices. Its operations and capital decisions are guided by shareholder approvals at annual general meetings, where mandates related to dividends, share issuance and repurchases are regularly refreshed to support financial flexibility and shareholder returns.

Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Ltd. ( (HK:0232) ) has issued an announcement.

Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Limited has announced that its board has noticed unusual recent share price movements on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and, after inquiry, is unaware of any cause other than an ongoing strategic discussion. The company is in talks with an independent investor over a possible disposal transaction that could trigger obligations under Hong Kong’s Takeovers Code and has begun an offer period from 29 May 2026, requiring monthly updates and heightened disclosure of dealings by associates and intermediaries.

The group confirms it currently has over 9.3 billion shares in issue and no other relevant securities under the Takeovers Code, and it has reminded major shareholders, the potential investor’s associates, and market intermediaries of their duty to report trades in its securities. The announcement signals a potentially significant change in ownership structure, increases regulatory scrutiny around trading activity, and may affect stakeholder expectations as the market awaits clarity on whether a formal offer or disposal will proceed.

More about Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Ltd.

Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries in the aerospace technology sector. The group focuses on aviation-related products and services, positioning itself within the broader aerospace industry and targeting global aircraft and aviation markets.

Media Chinese International Ltd ( (HK:0685) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Media Chinese International Limited has issued a clarification regarding its annual results announcement for the year ended 31 March 2026, stating that an earlier section referring to the closure of the register of members for an interim dividend was included in error and should be deleted in full. The board confirmed that it has not recommended any final or interim dividend for the financial year, and all other information in the previously released annual results remains unchanged.

Following the clarification, the company arranged for the resumption of trading in its shares, which had been halted on both the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and Bursa Malaysia pending the release of this announcement. Trading in Media Chinese International’s shares was set to resume on the same day on both exchanges, helping to restore normal market activity and provide clarity to shareholders regarding dividend expectations and the company’s disclosure.

More about Media Chinese International Ltd

Media Chinese International Limited is a Hong Kong-listed media company with shares also traded on Bursa Malaysia, focusing on Chinese-language publishing and related media operations across its key markets. The group serves investors in both Hong Kong and Malaysia, and its shares are identified by stock codes 685 in Hong Kong and 5090 in Malaysia.
